concept: Sail / Wind-Catching Cloth / To Travel by Wind
description: Cloth spread to catch wind for propelling a vessel; to travel using wind power. 'Phora' names what catches breath—fabric that transforms wind into journey.
sound_symbolism: The word breathes wind. 'Pho' /ɸo/ fills with gentle breath, sail billowing. Then 'ra' reaches forward, vessel moving with wind.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo phora' (the sail). As verb: 'mi phora' (I sail). Ancient renewable energy—wind becoming motion.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sails carried civilization—trade, exploration, migration, all powered by patient wind.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Moving with forces—the sail doesn't fight wind but uses it, cooperation with nature.
